title: Flavorful Middle Eastern Lentil Mujadara
description: A herbal and cozy Middle Eastern dish featuring lentils and egg rolls, served in a scallop shell vessel, with a wood-fired bake flavor and low-carb friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot, bring 2 cups of water to a boil and add the lentils, reducing heat to a simmer and cooking until tender.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked lentils, onion mixture, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper, and mix well.
Lay an egg roll wrapper on a flat surface and place about 1/4 cup of the lentil mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with water and fold the wrapper into a triangle, pressing the edges together to seal.
Place the filled wrappers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Serve the lentil mujadara in scallop shell vessels, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
